rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical coupling involving anodic oxidation of arenes/thiophenes/styrenes, studied by cyclic voltammetry to establish oxidation potentials of substrates
electrochemical_role: Anodic single-electron oxidation of electron-rich (hetero)arenes and styrenes to their radical cations at a glassy carbon electrode (E_p/2 ranging from 1.26 V to 2.19 V vs SCE)
electron_flow: The anode performs single-electron oxidation of the electron-rich (hetero)aromatic substrate to a radical cation; subsequent chemistry (e.g., deprotonation to a benzylic radical) is probed by radical trapping experiments.

probe_reagents.name: benzylic radical trap (TEMPO or similar, as indicated by 'Benzylic radical trapping experiment' section)
probe_reagents.observation: Section header indicates a benzylic radical trapping experiment was performed to evidence a benzylic radical intermediate (details on subsequent pages not shown)

notes: Only the CV portion and the header of a benzylic radical trapping experiment are visible. CV data (E_p/2: compound 49 = 1.26 V; compound 2 = 1.70 V; compound 47 = 1.46 V; compound 1 = 2.00 V; compound 3 = 1.71 V; 2-acetylthiophene = 2.19 V) rank substrate oxidation potentials, supporting that electron-rich heteroaromatics/styrenes are oxidized first under anodic conditions. Probe reagent identity for the benzylic radical trapping experiment is not visible on this page.
